task_completion.visual_evidence: A web browser is open and navigated to https://cameochemicals.noaa.gov/.

task_completion.description: Search for the candidate solvents on CAMEO Chemicals
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots showing the agent viewing the chemical datasheets or search results for the candidate solvents (Benzene, Acetone, Dimethyl Sulfoxide, Methylene Chloride, or Acetophenone) on the CAMEO Chemicals website.
A text editor is open, editing a file named 'solvent_screening_matrix.txt' located in the '/home/ga/Documents/' directory.

task_completion.description: Record the Flash Point and Water Solubility data in the report
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text editor shows the recorded Flash Point and Water Solubility values for the evaluated candidate solvents.

task_completion.description: Identify the safer substitute based on the criteria
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text file explicitly names Dimethyl Sulfoxide (or DMSO) as the safer substitute, winner, or the only solvent that passes all criteria.

integrity.description: The agent used a web browser to search for the chemicals
integrity.visual_evidence: The web browser UI is visible with search queries or chemical pages from CAMEO Chemicals, rather than using terminal commands to fetch data.

integrity.description: The agent used a GUI text editor to create the report
integrity.visual_evidence: A graphical text editor is used to write the report, rather than terminal commands like 'echo' or 'cat'.

integrity.description: The agent recorded authentic data from the website
integrity.visual_evidence: The recorded values in the text file match the real CAMEO Chemicals data (e.g., DMSO Flash Point 203°F, Acetone Flash Point 0°F) and are not hallucinated.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Dimethyl Sulfoxide (DMSO) is the correct safer substitute. It passes all criteria: Flash Point is 203°F, Water Solubility is Miscible (≥100 mg/mL), and it is not listed as a carcinogen. Benzene fails all three. Acetone fails Fire Safety (Flash Point 0°F). Methylene Chloride fails Health Safety (carcinogen) and Process Efficiency (slightly soluble). Acetophenone fails Process Efficiency (slightly soluble/unavailable). The final report must be located at /home/ga/Documents/solvent_screening_matrix.txt.
